message:
refactor(anthropic): migrate retry logic to shared HttpRetryConfig and
migrate WASI tests from tokio to fcp-async-core

anthropic/client.rs:
- Replace hand-rolled max_retries/initial_delay_ms/max_delay_ms fields
  with HttpRetryConfig from fcp-sdk::migration
- Import RetryLoop, AttemptOutcome, ExecutionContext for the shared
  retry infrastructure
- Remove unused fcp_async_core::time::sleep and tracing::warn imports
- with_retry_config() now constructs HttpRetryConfig struct

fcp-sandbox/no_mock_integration.rs:
- Replace 4 #[tokio::test(flavor = "current_thread")] attributes with
  #[fcp_async_core::runtime::test] to align WASI integration tests
  with the async-core runtime abstraction

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.2","title":"[FCP3.CUTOVER] Remove reqwest/Tokio/compat holdouts, exception ledger, and migration scaffolding","description":"## Goal
Remove remaining compatibility-first runtime and transport dependencies after the new path is proven.

## Why this exists
The redesign does not really land until the quarantine layers disappear. Leaving old async, transport, and migration scaffolding in place would preserve the old mental model and make future regressions more likely.

## Scope
- reqwest-heavy shim layers where native transport or client stacks replace them
- `tokio-tungstenite` and other holdout transports where the FCP3 design supersedes them
- `axum` or `hyper` compatibility centrality where the host architecture no longer requires it
- exception ledger entries, migration scaffolding, and temporary compatibility glue whose job is complete
- verification that deleted holdouts are covered by the new conformance, performance, and evidence program

## Acceptance criteria
The surviving runtime and transport stack reflects deliberate FCP3 design choices rather than historical leftovers, and no compatibility-first holdout remains because "maybe we still need it later."","notes":"## FCP3 execution note
This bead is part of the FCP3 re-foundation and must remain self-contained. Future implementers should not need to consult the original planning conversation to understand intent, scope, architectural posture, or why the work matters.

## No-compat rule
Do not preserve backwards compatibility, dual-stack behavior, fallback paths, or migration-era abstractions beyond the minimum temporary quarantine explicitly called out elsewhere in the graph. Once a replacement path is proven, the old path should be deleted.

## Verification and evidence mandate
- Planning/spec/design beads must explicitly define the downstream verification story rather than hand-wave it.
- Implementation beads are not complete without focused unit/property tests, negative/failure-path coverage, and where relevant failure-injection coverage for cancellation, drain, restart, timeout, backpressure, failover, and recovery behavior.
- Host- or runtime-crossing changes must include end-to-end scripts with detailed structured logging, timestamps, digests/ids, receipts or health outcomes, and clear replay instructions.
- Evidence must be redaction-safe, easy to inspect, and sufficient for a future engineer to diagnose failures without re-deriving the intended behavior.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:54:44.164245Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T21:22:07.432101Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compat","cutover","fcp3","tokio"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:54:44.164245Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:56:10.986339Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:56:11.123127Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:56:11.261006Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"closed_reason":"FCP3 CUTOVER compat holdouts verified removed. fcp-streaming close() fixed."}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.3","title":"[FCP3.CUTOVER] Bulk connector-family migration by archetype onto the FCP3 SDK and ABI","description":"## Goal
Migrate the connector fleet by archetype onto the FCP3 SDK and ABI after pilots prove the model.

## Why this exists
The repository should converge deliberately, not drift through mixed paradigms. Grouping migration by connector family makes deletion opportunities, testing expectations, and rollout logic explicit.

## Scope
- group connectors by archetype and migration complexity
- prioritize families that delete the most compatibility code and prove the most reusable patterns
- force every migration wave to produce unit, integration, and replayable e2e evidence rather than hand-waving parity
- keep the migration sequence self-documenting so remaining legacy surfaces are always shrinking, never stabilizing

## Acceptance criteria
Every connector family migration wave has clear preconditions, evidence expectations, and deletion outcomes, so the cutover path remains auditable and irreversible.","notes":"## FCP3 execution note
This bead is part of the FCP3 re-foundation and must remain self-contained. Future implementers should not need to consult the original planning conversation to understand intent, scope, architectural posture, or why the work matters.

## No-compat rule
Do not preserve backwards compatibility, dual-stack behavior, fallback paths, or migration-era abstractions beyond the minimum temporary quarantine explicitly called out elsewhere in the graph. Once a replacement path is proven, the old path should be deleted.

## Verification and evidence mandate
- Planning/spec/design beads must explicitly define the downstream verification story rather than hand-wave it.
- Implementation beads are not complete without focused unit/property tests, negative/failure-path coverage, and where relevant failure-injection coverage for cancellation, drain, restart, timeout, backpressure, failover, and recovery behavior.
- Host- or runtime-crossing changes must include end-to-end scripts with detailed structured logging, timestamps, digests/ids, receipts or health outcomes, and clear replay instructions.
- Evidence must be redaction-safe, easy to inspect, and sufficient for a future engineer to diagnose failures without re-deriving the intended behavior.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:54:44.330708Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T21:14:57.529446Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","cutover","fcp3","migration"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:54:44.330708Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:56:11.400251Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:56:11.536394Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.3.1","title":"[FCP3.MIGRATE] Request-response connector family migration wave","description":"## Goal
Migrate the request-response connector families onto the FCP3 SDK and ABI after the request-response pilot proves the model.

## Why this exists
This should be the first broad migration wave because it offers the fastest deletion wins and the clearest chance to validate the common-case authoring story before more complex connector families move.

## Scope
- group low-state, RPC-style connectors into ordered migration batches
- prefer families that let us delete the most compatibility code fastest
- require unit, host-backed integration, and replayable e2e evidence with detailed logs for each migrated family
- record intentional behavior changes instead of hiding them as accidental drift

## Acceptance criteria
A completed batch leaves less compatibility code behind, has replayable evidence for success and failure paths, and improves the default connector authoring story for the rest of the migration program.","notes":"## FCP3 execution note
This bead is part of the FCP3 re-foundation and must remain self-contained. Future implementers should not need to consult the original planning conversation to understand intent, scope, architectural posture, or why the work matters.

- Implementation beads are not complete without focused unit/property tests, negative/failure-path coverage, and where relevant failure-injection coverage for cancellation, drain, restart, timeout, backpressure, failover, and recovery behavior.
- Host- or runtime-crossing changes must include end-to-end scripts with detailed structured logging, timestamps, digests/ids, receipts or health outcomes, and clear replay instructions.
- Evidence must be redaction-safe, easy to inspect, and sufficient for a future engineer to diagnose failures without re-deriving the intended behavior.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:04:19.680377Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T21:22:07.445190Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp3","migration","request-response"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.3.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:04:55.347647Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.3.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:04:55.208914Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.3.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:04:19.680377Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.3.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:04:55.486377Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.3.2","title":"[FCP3.MIGRATE] Streaming connector family migration wave","description":"## Goal
Migrate the streaming connector families onto the FCP3 SDK and ABI after the streaming pilot proves the model.

## Why this exists
Streaming connectors are where transport semantics, backpressure, cancellation, reconnect, and operator visibility are easiest to get subtly wrong. This wave should apply the pilot lessons systematically across the fleet.

## Scope
- prioritize connectors with WebSocket, SSE, or long-lived bidirectional semantics
- require proof of backpressure, cancel, reconnect, drain, and recovery behavior under the new model
- require unit, integration, and replayable e2e evidence with detailed logs for each migrated family
- delete prior transport glue once each family is migrated and verified

## Acceptance criteria
Each migrated streaming family demonstrates the FCP3 transport and lifecycle model in production-shaped scenarios rather than only passing narrow happy-path tests.","notes":"## FCP3 execution note
This bead is part of the FCP3 re-foundation and must remain self-contained. Future implementers should not need to consult the original planning conversation to understand intent, scope, architectural posture, or why the work matters.

## No-compat rule
Do not preserve backwards compatibility, dual-stack behavior, fallback paths, or migration-era abstractions beyond the minimum temporary quarantine explicitly called out elsewhere in the graph. Once a replacement path is proven, the old path should be deleted.

## Verification and evidence mandate
- Planning/spec/design beads must explicitly define the downstream verification story rather than hand-wave it.
- Implementation beads are not complete without focused unit/property tests, negative/failure-path coverage, and where relevant failure-injection coverage for cancellation, drain, restart, timeout, backpressure, failover, and recovery behavior.
- Host- or runtime-crossing changes must include end-to-end scripts with detailed structured logging, timestamps, digests/ids, receipts or health outcomes, and clear replay instructions.
- Evidence must be redaction-safe, easy to inspect, and sufficient for a future engineer to diagnose failures without re-deriving the intended behavior.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:04:19.828865Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T21:22:07.718088Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp3","migration","streaming"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.3.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.10.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:04:55.762324Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.3.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:04:55.626229Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.3.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:04:19.828865Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.3.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:04:55.898183Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.3.3","title":"[FCP3.MIGRATE] Polling, webhook, and other stateful connector family migration wave","description":"## Goal
Migrate the polling, webhook, and other stateful or background connector families onto the FCP3 SDK and ABI after the stateful pilot proves the model.

## Why this exists
These connectors combine durability, scheduling, replay risk, and background lifecycle complexity. They should migrate only once the stateful patterns are well-proven and the evidence model is strong enough to diagnose subtle failures.

## Scope
- cursor-based polling, webhook receivers, and background-service-heavy connectors
- explicit proof of durable state, failover, drain, retry, idempotency, and receipt behavior
- unit, integration, and replayable e2e evidence with detailed logs for each migrated family
- deletion of old lifecycle or runtime glue after each family is migrated

## Acceptance criteria
A completed stateful migration batch leaves behind a connector family that is safer, more observable, and easier to operate than its pre-FCP3 version, with no fallback to old lifecycle glue.","notes":"## FCP3 execution note
This bead is part of the FCP3 re-foundation and must remain self-contained. Future implementers should not need to consult the original planning conversation to understand intent, scope, architectural posture, or why the work matters.

## No-compat rule
Do not preserve backwards compatibility, dual-stack behavior, fallback paths, or migration-era abstractions beyond the minimum temporary quarantine explicitly called out elsewhere in the graph. Once a replacement path is proven, the old path should be deleted.

## Verification and evidence mandate
- Planning/spec/design beads must explicitly define the downstream verification story rather than hand-wave it.
- Implementation beads are not complete without focused unit/property tests, negative/failure-path coverage, and where relevant failure-injection coverage for cancellation, drain, restart, timeout, backpressure, failover, and recovery behavior.
- Host- or runtime-crossing changes must include end-to-end scripts with detailed structured logging, timestamps, digests/ids, receipts or health outcomes, and clear replay instructions.
- Evidence must be redaction-safe, easy to inspect, and sufficient for a future engineer to diagnose failures without re-deriving the intended behavior.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:04:19.987575Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T21:22:07.737374Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp3","migration","polling","webhook"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.3.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.10.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:04:56.172156Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.3.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:04:56.034231Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.3.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:04:19.987575Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.3.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:04:56.312690Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.12","title":"[FCP3] Documentation alignment: README, AGENTS guidance, crate docs, and migration notes","description":"# [FCP3] Documentation Alignment

## Goal
Ensure README, AGENTS guidance, crate docs, runbooks, troubleshooting guides, and migration notes all reflect FCP3-only reality once the redesign direction is locked.

## Why this exists
A clean implementation still fails users if the docs teach the wrong mental model. This bead exists to make the public and internal guidance match the real system, including how to provision connectors, interpret denials, run diagnostics, and replay failures.

## Scope
- README architecture and onboarding descriptions
- AGENTS.md project guidance where FCP3 changes repo workflow or architectural truth
- crate and module docs that still describe compatibility-first behavior
- runbooks, troubleshooting material, and operator-oriented reference docs for explain, drain, repair, and replay flows
- elimination of stale wording that implies old execution models remain blessed

## Acceptance criteria
A future user, contributor, or agent can learn how FCP3 works and how to operate it from the docs without being pulled back toward deprecated mental models.","notes":"## FCP3 execution note
This bead is part of the FCP3 re-foundation and must remain self-contained. Future implementers should not need to consult the original planning conversation to understand intent, scope, architectural posture, or why the work matters.

## No-compat rule
Do not preserve backwards compatibility, dual-stack behavior, fallback paths, or migration-era abstractions beyond the minimum temporary quarantine explicitly called out elsewhere in the graph. Once a replacement path is proven, the old path should be deleted.

## Verification and evidence mandate
- Planning/spec/design beads must explicitly define the downstream verification story rather than hand-wave it.
- Implementation beads are not complete without focused unit/property tests, negative/failure-path coverage, and where relevant failure-injection coverage for cancellation, drain, restart, timeout, backpressure, failover, and recovery behavior.
- Host- or runtime-crossing changes must include end-to-end scripts with detailed structured logging, timestamps, digests/ids, receipts or health outcomes, and clear replay instructions.
- Evidence must be redaction-safe, easy to inspect, and sufficient for a future engineer to diagnose failures without re-deriving the intended behavior.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:52:53.144753Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T21:23:00.672152Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["agents","docs","fcp3","readme"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.12","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:52:53.144753Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.12","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:56:03.516435Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.12","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:56:11.676409Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.12","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.10.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:13:56.059383Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.12","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:56:03.650578Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.12","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.11.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:56:11.814552Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.12","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.14.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:04:56.449848Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.12","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T21:13:55.480127Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.12","depends_on_id":"flyw  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.13","title":"[FCP3] Portable connector execution and mesh placement across local, WASI, and remote nodes","description":"# [FCP3] Portable Connector Execution and Mesh Placement

## Goal
Define how connector apps are placed, moved, resumed, failed over, and fenced across local, WASI, and remote mesh execution environments under the FCP3 model.

## Why this matters
FCP is not only a local host/runtime redesign. The platform vision includes zone-aware execution across a trusted mesh. This deserves a first-class workstream rather than being buried as a footnote under policy or transport mapping.

## Scope
- placement planner and device/zone fitness model
- local versus remote execution selection rules
- leases, checkpoints, and failover semantics for connector apps with state or singleton constraints
- interaction with host supervision, manifest constraints, and transport/addressing","notes":"## FCP3 execution note
This bead is part of the FCP3 re-foundation and must remain self-contained. Future implementers should not need to consult the original planning conversation to understand intent, scope, architectural posture, or why the work matters.

## No-compat rule
Do not preserve backwards compatibility, dual-stack behavior, fallback paths, or migration-era abstractions beyond the minimum temporary quarantine explicitly called out elsewhere in the graph. Once a replacement path is proven, the old path should be deleted.

## Verification and evidence mandate
- Planning/spec/design beads must explicitly define the downstream verification story rather than hand-wave it.
- Implementation beads are not complete without focused unit/property tests, negative/failure-path coverage, and where relevant failure-injection coverage for cancellation, drain, restart, timeout, backpressure, failover, and recovery behavior.
- Host- or runtime-crossing changes must include end-to-end scripts with detailed structured logging, timestamps, digests/ids, receipts or health outcomes, and clear replay instructions.
- Evidence must be redaction-safe, easy to inspect, and sufficient for a future engineer to diagnose failures without re-deriving the intended behavior.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:56:36.532317Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-12T23:13:47.998969Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T23:13:47.998665Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["execution","fcp3","mesh","placement"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.13","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:56:36.532317Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.13","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:56:48.474833Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.13","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:56:48.629539Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.13","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:56:48.285796Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.13","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9syku.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-09T20:56:48.767745Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
